content.text:
[
  {
    "ruleId": "V-RHYTHM",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "section 'What does a taste look like as numbers?', three separate sentences",
    "evidence": "'A prediction you can be wrong about is worth ten definitions you nod along to.' ... 'Similarity, it turns out, has a basement.' ... 'The number measures which way two arrows point, not what the things are.'",
    "suggestion": "Keep the strongest of the three (the direction/basement pairing) and let the other land as plain explanation; one mic-drop per section, not three."
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V-REGISTER",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "beat 1, paragraph 4",
    "evidence": "'One answer to it runs half of modern machine learning.'",
    "suggestion": "'One answer to it shows up all over modern machine learning' or drop the sweeping fraction and let the build itself carry the stakes."
  }
]

```json
[
  {
    "ruleId": "V-DASH",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "where": "'What does a taste look like as numbers?' section, paragraph beginning 'Watch again, more carefully.'",
    "evidence": "'So the score can't be reading sameness of items; it's reading agreement of direction.'",
    "suggestion": "'So the score can't be reading sameness of items. It's reading agreement of direction.'"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V-DASH",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "where": "'Same meter, different aisle' section, paragraph beginning 'Now keep the same meter running'",
    "evidence": "'It's you, the one asking the question; the role is the candidate you're scoring.'",
    "suggestion": "'It's you, the one asking the question. The role is the candidate you're scoring.'"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V-DASH",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "where": "'What is the machine you just used?' section, second 'Honest edges' callout",
    "evidence": "'you still multiply entry by entry and add, there are just more entries'",
    "suggestion": "'you still multiply entry by entry and add. There are just more entries.'"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V-REGISTER",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "opening paragraph ('The score behind the song') and closing paragraph ('Same meter, different aisle')",
    "evidence": "'two lists of numbers got compared, and the comparison said: same taste' / 'two lists of numbers got compared, and the verdict was same taste'",
    "suggestion": "consider active phrasing, e.g. 'something compared two lists of numbers and called it: same taste,' though the passive may be intentional for the faceless-machine effect"
  }
]
```

content.text:
[
  {
    "ruleId": "V-REGISTER",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "'The score behind the song' section, sentence 3",
    "evidence": "'Somewhere in a data center two descriptions got compared.'",
    "suggestion": "'Somewhere in a data center, two descriptions matched up.'"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V-RHYTHM",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "'The honest look' section, sentences 3-5",
    "evidence": "'It doubles, exactly. 4.2 becomes 8.4, to the last digit. Double one input, double the output: the meter is linear in each arrow you feed it... Feed it an arrow twice as long and you get back precisely twice the score, no surprise hiding in the arithmetic.'",
    "suggestion": "Cut one of the three restatements; the doubling point is made and re-made without new information between them."
  }
]
